pi_items.source: Task setup script and panoply_env install script downloading real NOAA PSL NCEP Reanalysis datasets
setup_task.sh instructions and export_result.sh / verifier.py logic
verifier.py sets acceptable lower bound for low SLP to 970 <= low_val <= 1015
verifier.py sets acceptable upper bound for high SLP to 1015 <= high_val <= 1060

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a Combine Plot and a standalone Sea Level Pressure (SLP) plot using Panoply with real NCEP/NCAR Reanalysis datasets downloaded from NOAA. The agent must document the SLP for a major climatological Low and High in January. While the task metadata claims valid SLP ranges of [990, 1015] hPa and [1015, 1050] hPa, the grading script (verifier.py) explicitly enforces broader tolerances of [970, 1015] hPa and [1015, 1060] hPa, contradicting the metadata. Furthermore, the task instructions explicitly mislead the agent by falsely stating the data is in Pascals and must be divided by 100; however, the actual netCDF data is in millibars (which are equal to hPa). An agent that blindly follows this instruction instead of observing the true data units in Panoply will fail the verifier's physical plausibility check.
